assertions.text: Table has at least one column name ending with '?' (e.g. 'Discount?' or 'Expected?')
Table has exactly 3 data rows (GOLD, SILVER, BRONZE)
assertions.evidence:
Gold customer   | GOLD   | 20
    Silver customer | SILVER | 10
    Bronze customer | BRONZE | 0

assertions.text: Test method body contains only one assertEquals (or equivalent assertion) — not three
Test method body contains no if or switch statements
Table has a scenario/description column as the leftmost column
Test method has either a @DisplayName annotation or a method name that reads as a clear, descriptive title when converted from camelCase/snake_case
assertions.evidence: void calculatesDiscountByTier(String tier, int discount) — converts to 'Calculates Discount By Tier'

assertions.text: @Description is present and provides context beyond what the table rows already express — such as fixed values shared by all rows, where/when the rule applies, or open questions. Does NOT merely restate the column names or summarise what the rows show.
assertions.evidence: No @Description annotation appears anywhere in the model response. The response contains only @TableTest and the method body.

assertions.text: Annotations appear in order: @DisplayName (if present), @Description (if present), @TableTest — not any other order.
assertions.evidence: Only @TableTest is present in the response; neither @DisplayName nor @Description appear, so the ordering constraint is trivially satisfied.

assertions.text: @Description text uses a text block (triple-quoted string """) when the text is long, not string concatenation with +.
assertions.evidence: No @Description annotation is present in the model response at all, so there is no @Description text to evaluate for text block usage.
